title: Fix internvl fp16 error

step_plan.summary: Modify intern_attention_forward to promote numerically-sensitive attention computations (dot-products and softmax) to float32 and cast the resulting attention output back to the module's original dtype to fix fp16 numerical instability while keeping external dtype behavior unchanged.

steps.operation: Promote attention computations to float32 inside intern_attention_forward and cast results back to the module's original dtype
steps.reason: Softmax and scaled dot-product operations are numerically unstable in fp16/bfloat16. Promote q/k/v and all intermediate attention computations (dot-products, softmax, scaling) to float32 to avoid NaNs/Infs and poor precision; then cast the computed attention output back to the original dtype before the final projection to preserve the module's external dtype behavior. Also ensure the fused scaled_dot_product_attention branch uses float32 inputs/outputs when necessary.
steps.side_effects: Slight runtime and memory overhead due to dtype conversions and increased temporary buffer size during attention (may be more noticeable on large sequence lengths). Numerical outputs for fp16 runs will change (improved stability and potentially small differences vs previous fp16 results); fp32 behavior remains unchanged. Downstream callers and public API are not changed, but unit/integration tests must be added/updated to check for finite outputs (no NaNs/Infs) in fp16 and to validate similarity to fp32 references. If the runtime uses vendor/fused attention kernels that already handle internal promotion, behavior should remain compatible; however, ensure compatibility with those kernels (they may accept float32 inputs or perform internal casting).
